title: fix: remove KV secondary storage for API key verification
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Fresh keys, no waiting
gist.blurb: We've rewired how API keys are verified, skipping the middleman cache to fix stale permission bugs. Your device logins are now instantly recognized and authenticated.
gist.summary: Removed KV secondary storage caching for API key verification in favor of direct D1 database queries to resolve stale permission issues.
gist.impact: API key authentication is now more reliable, and device flow logins will immediately reflect the correct permissions without caching delays.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee holding a glowing API key, flying directly to a database server, while the Nomnom creature eats old 'cache' boxes. Represents bypassing the cache for direct, fresh database reads.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot holding a glowing golden key, flying directly toward a glowing lime green (#ecf874) database server. The round tan nomnom creature happily eats old dusty cardboard boxes labeled 'CACHE'. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
